scope: Billing run termination: terminate button, status 'in progress termination' (termination allowed for runs in INITIAL, IN_PROGRESS_DRAFT, DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS_GENERATION, GENERATED, PAUSED); flow from UI/API to cancel() and stored procedure.
summary: Cross-dependencies for billing run termination (terminate button, PATCH /billing-run/terminate, BillingRunService.cancel(), terminate_billing_run stored procedure, lock cleanup, status CANCELLED).
entry_points: UI: Billing run list/detail – Terminate button (calls PATCH /billing-run/terminate)

upstream.name: billing_run (table/schema)
upstream.usage: Billing run entity; status updated to CANCELLED; processStage not updated by cancel()

upstream.name: billing_run.terminate_billing_run(?)
upstream.usage: Stored procedure called with run ID; may perform cleanup (lock release inconsistent per reports)

upstream.name: Lock / lock table
upstream.usage: Locks associated with billing run (billingId); terminate_billing_run may not always release them

upstream.name: BillingRunRepository
upstream.usage: save(billingRun) after status set to CANCELLED

upstream.name: BillingStatus enum
upstream.usage: availableStatusesForTermination: INITIAL, IN_PROGRESS_DRAFT, DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS_GENERATION, GENERATED, PAUSED; cancel() throws if status not in list

downstream.name: Billing run list/detail screen
downstream.usage: Terminate button triggers PATCH /billing-run/terminate; shows status (e.g. CANCELLED)

downstream.name: BillingRunStartAccountingScheduler / state listener
downstream.usage: Processes runs by status; resume() does not check CANCELLED, only processStage – cancelled runs can be resumed to IN_PROGRESS_ACCOUNTING

downstream.name: BillingRunService.resume()
downstream.usage: If processStage is ACCOUNTING/DRAFT, can set status back to IN_PROGRESS_ACCOUNTING/IN_PROGRESS_DRAFT; CANCELLED run can thus move back to in-progress

downstream.name: GET /billing-run/list, GET /billing-run/{id}
downstream.usage: Consumers expect CANCELLED status and correct lock state after termination

shared: BillingStatus enum (INITIAL, IN_PROGRESS_DRAFT, DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS_GENERATION, GENERATED, PAUSED, CANCELLED, COMPLETED, etc.)
billing_run schema and terminate_billing_run stored procedure
integration_points: UI Terminate button → PATCH /billing-run/terminate → BillingRunService.cancel() → permission check → availableStatusesForTermination check → session.doWork: CALL billing_run.terminate_billing_run(?) → billingRun.setStatus(CANCELLED) → save

Termination vs scheduler: cancel() does not update processStage; BillingRunStartAccountingScheduler and resume() can later move CANCELLED run to IN_PROGRESS_ACCOUNTING if processStage still ACCOUNTING
Lock lifecycle: billing run creates locks during processing; terminate_billing_run expected to release; observed inconsistency (some CANCELLED runs keep locks)
Regression: completion flow uses make_billing_run_real(?) and releases locks; termination uses terminate_billing_run(?); both must leave run in consistent state

what_could_break.reason: Stored procedure may not always unlock; CANCELLED runs can retain locks (see BillingRunLockAnalysis report); any change to terminate flow must preserve or add explicit unlock

resume() checks processStage not status; CANCELLED run with processStage ACCOUNTING can be resumed to IN_PROGRESS_ACCOUNTING and then COMPLETED; fix may require resume() to reject CANCELLED or clear processStage on cancel
Callers expect run to be CANCELLED and no longer processed; UI and batch jobs depend on status
Race if user terminates while start is in progress; ordering and validation of status transitions
Filters and counts by status; CANCELLED must be reflected; any new status (e.g. IN_PROGRESS_TERMINATION) would need UI and API support

technical_details: Rule 35a merge lookup: (1) PDT-2023: Local git was searched in Phoenix repos under Cursor-Project (phoenix-billing-run, phoenix-core, phoenix-core-lib, phoenix-ui, phoenix-api-gateway). git log --all --grep='PDT-2023' and branch names containing 'PDT' or '2023' returned no matches. Phoenix directories may be submodules or shallow clones (.git not found at phoenix-billing-run path). (2) No merge commit or MR identified for Jira key PDT-2023 on dev/dev2. Conditional sync: SKIPPED (no merge found for PDT-2023). (3) Scope derived from user description and existing reports: billing run termination, terminate button, statuses allowing termination (IN_PROGRESS_DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS_GENERATION, etc.). Key code references (from workspace reports): BillingRunService.cancel() (lines ~2475-2513), availableStatusesForTermination = INITIAL, IN_PROGRESS_DRAFT, DRAFT, IN_PROGRESS_GENERATION, GENERATED, PAUSED; CALL billing_run.terminate_billing_run(?); billingRun.setStatus(BillingStatus.CANCELLED); processStage not updated. BillingRunStartAccountingService (completion) and BillingRunStartAccountingScheduler (resume/IN_PROGRESS_ACCOUNTING) are downstream. Modules: phoenix-billing-run (BillingRunService, BillingRunRepository), billing_run schema, Lock entity.
